issue.location: final sentence of the introductory gate-flow paragraph
before_problem: The round46 page still split the transition phrase as 'broad ro- / bustness claims.'
The split occurred immediately before the contribution list, making the first evidence-chain story look less polished.
rejected_candidate: The phrase 'not a blanket claim' removed the robustness split but left a one-word 'claim.' line, so it was rejected.

change_summary.before: Together, these gates support bounded claims, not broad robustness claims.
change_summary.after: Together, these gates bound the paper's claim.
change_summary.summary: Shortened the boundary sentence to remove the page-2 ro-/bustness split and avoid a one-word continuation line.

observations: The page-2 before/after crop shows the prior 'broad ro- / bustness claims' split removed.
The accepted sentence appears as 'Together, these gates / bound the paper's claim' without an isolated one-word line.
Contribution item 3 now begins at the bottom of the left column; Related Work remains clean at the top of the right column.
The full contact sheet remains 11 pages with no obvious figure/table collision or blank page.
